incorrects.description: Student selected 5: subtracted instead of adding (14 - 9 = 5)

steps.text: The question says together. That means add Maya's 14 books and Ben's 9 books. What's 14 plus 9?

incorrects.description: Student selected 14: chose Maya's value only, didn't add Ben

steps.text: You found Maya. But the question asks for Maya AND Ben together. Add both amounts.

incorrects.description: Student selected 22: misread scale or miscounted

steps.text: Check the scale. Each mark counts by 2. Maya is at 14, Ben is at 9. Count carefully and add those values.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation demonstrates: Maya bar at 14, Ben bar at 9. 14 + 9 = 23.

steps.text: Let me show you. Maya read 14 books. I find that by counting up by 2s to where her bar stops. Ben read 9. Together means add: 14 plus 9 equals 23. The answer is 23 books together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34: added all values instead of subtracting (23 + 11 = 34)

steps.text: How many MORE means compare. We subtract. Maya and Ben together is 23. Carla is 11. What's 23 minus 11?

incorrects.description: Student selected 23: gave the combined total without subtracting Carla

steps.text: That's the combined total. But the question asks how many MORE than Carla. Compare 23 to Carla's 11. Subtract.

incorrects.description: Student selected 17: used incorrect combined total or subtraction error

params.description: Maya, Ben, and Carla bars highlight.

steps.text: Check your work. Maya and Ben together is 23. Carla is 11. To find how many more, subtract: 23 minus 11. What's that difference?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation demonstrates: combined total 23 displayed, Carla bar at 11 highlighted, calculation 23 - 11 = 12 shown.

steps.text: Here's how this works. Maya and Ben together read 23 books. Carla read 11. How many MORE means we compare by subtracting: 23 minus 11 equals 12. Maya and Ben together read 12 more books than Carla.
